data.title: Market Correction May Exceed Expectations Due to Unmet Macro and Policy Factors
data.description: Yili Hua has indicated that the current market correction could surpass initial projections due to unmet macroeconomic and policy expectations. According to NS3.AI, factors such as unfulfilled Federal Reserve rate-cut expectations, ongoing Middle East conflicts, delayed cryptocurrency reform bills, and disappointment regarding previously discussed expectations, including a Bitcoin national strategy, are contributing to this situation.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Discusses Crypto Investor Categories
data.description: Liquid Capital founder Yili Hua has categorized crypto investors into three distinct groups. According to NS3.AI, Hua identifies traditional finance funds as the most passive winners in the crypto market. He recounted advising a traditional investor in 2018 to allocate approximately 5% of their portfolio to Bitcoin (BTC) and to hold it long-term, avoiding trading in altcoins.

data.title: Aethir Blocks Vulnerability Attack, Plans Compensation for Users
data.description: Aethir has successfully thwarted a vulnerability attack on its bridge contract, estimating user losses to be around $90,000. According to NS3.AI, the company assured that ATH on Ethereum remains secure. Aethir is set to announce a compensation plan for affected users next week.

data.title: AI TRENDS | China's AI Industry Alliance Issues Risk Management Guide for Enterprise Security
data.description: China's AI industry alliance has introduced a risk management guide aimed at enhancing enterprise security governance, similar to OpenClaw. According to NS3.AI, the guide establishes a security framework and self-inspection standards for the deployment, usage, and decommissioning of AI agents.

data.title: The "Guide to Risk Management of OpenClaw-like Intelligent Agent Deployment" has been officially released.
data.description: During the 17th Plenary Session of the China Artificial Intelligence Industry Development Alliance (CAAIIA), the CAAIIA Security Governance Committee officially released the "Guideline for Risk Management of OpenClaw-like Intelligent Agent Deployment." To systematically address the security challenges posed by OpenClaw-like intelligent agents, the guidelines focus on the pain points of risk prevention and control for enterprises during the technology implementation process. They propose an overall framework and self-inspection standards for secure deployment covering the entire process of deployment, use, and decommissioning, aiming to promote the development of intelligent agents from "being able to deploy" to "secure deployment, standardized use, and continuous governance," providing practical guidance for enterprises to conduct standardized technology deployment. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iranian parliamentary official: Israeli oil tankers are "absolutely prohibited" from passing through the Strait of Hormuz
data.description: The Iranian Students' News Agency reported that Ibrahim Aziz, chairman of the Iranian Parliament's National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, stated today that Israeli oil tankers are prohibited from passing through the Strait of Hormuz. He explicitly stated, "Israeli oil tankers are absolutely prohibited from passing through the Strait of Hormuz. Ships and cargo associated with the Zionist regime, whether military or civilian, are prohibited from passing through the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz." He also pointed out that other countries hostile to the Islamic Republic of Iran are similarly prohibited from passing through the strait. He added, "Hostile countries, as well as countries that cooperate with these countries and endanger Iran's security, have no right to pass through the Strait of Hormuz." (Jin Shi)

data.title: Market news: Iran will propose a permanent ban on oil tankers linked to the United States and Israel passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Market news: Ebrahim Azizi, chairman of the Iranian parliament's National Security Committee, stated that parliament has proposed a permanent ban on oil tankers with ties to the United States and Israel passing through the Strait of Hormuz. Ships with ties to or traveling to Israel will also be prohibited from passage, and the ban will also apply to countries that have taken action against the "Resistance Front." (Jinshi)

data.title: Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices rose in the short term, with WTI crude oil breaking through $100 per barrel.
data.description: Gate data shows that both WTI and Brent crude oil prices rose in the short term, with WTI crude oil breaking through $100 per barrel, up 2.04% on the day; Brent crude oil rose above $95 per barrel, up 1.15% on the day.

data.title: Iran Proposes Permanent Ban on U.S. and Israeli Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Iran's parliamentary National Security Committee Chairman, Ebrahim Azizi, has announced a proposal to permanently prohibit oil tankers linked to the United States and Israel from passing through the Strait of Hormuz. According to Odaily, vessels associated with or traveling to Israel will also face restrictions. The ban extends to countries taking actions against the 'Resistance Front.'

data.title: Yi Lihua: I remain bullish in the long term after experiencing multiple bull and bear markets, but we must respect the cycles and huge fluctuations.
data.description: During a live broadcast on Binance Square, Yi Lihua stated that he entered the industry at the end of 2015 and has experienced two or three bull and bear markets. From a long-term perspective, "long-term bullishness" is more advantageous, but one should not be overly optimistic and needs to respect the cyclical nature of the market and its "huge volatility".

data.title: Yi Lihua: Failure to meet macroeconomic and policy expectations, coupled with cyclical factors, may lead to a correction exceeding the original plan.
data.description: Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua stated in a Binance Square livestream that this year's market pressure is related to a confluence of factors, including the failure to meet expectations for a Federal Reserve interest rate cut, conflicts in the Middle East, the delayed passage of the crypto reform bill, and the disappointment of previously discussed expectations such as a "BTC national strategy." Coupled with the four-year cycle factor, he believes the current correction may exceed initial projections. However, he also emphasized that his long-term view on ETH remains unchanged, and that short-term volatility is largely due to external financial conditions and cycles.

data.title: Yi Lihua: Crypto market investors can be roughly divided into three categories, with the most "easy money" coming from traditional funds that don't require much effort.
data.description: Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua stated in a Binance Square livestream that the investors he encounters can be divided into several categories: one is the more "institutionalized" crypto VCs, whose research reports and frameworks seem complete, but lack a stable profit model after the trend fades, leading to significant survival pressure; another category consists of investors who specialize in specific sectors, possess professional barriers, and can generate stable cash flow; the third category comprises individuals from traditional finance who are more restrained in their circle of competence, often avoiding chasing trends and focusing on core asset allocation in the long term. He also shared a case study, mentioning that in 2018 he advised a traditional investor to allocate approximately 5% of their assets to BTC, which the investor held long-term without participating in altcoin trading, and subsequently, the proportion of this asset allocation increased significantly over time.

data.title: Oil Prices Rise Ahead of U.S.-Iran Talks, Remain Below $100 per Barrel
data.description:
Oil prices have increased but remain below $100 per barrel as the U.S. and Iran prepare for talks this weekend. According to Odaily, Emmanuel Belostrino, head of global crude oil and geopolitical market data at Kpler, stated that the outcome of the negotiations, particularly the possibility of establishing a viable shipping agreement, is crucial to alleviating the current backlog.

During early European trading, both U.S. and Brent crude saw short-term gains, with prices at $99.7 and $97.6 per barrel, respectively. The Strait of Hormuz remains largely frozen, causing supply disruptions and maintaining market tension. Additionally, Iran's attack on Saudi Arabia's main export route, the East-West pipeline, has reduced its daily capacity by approximately 700,000 barrels.

data.title: State Farm Settles Class Action for $20.93 Million Over Misleading Policies
data.description: State Farm has agreed to establish a $20.93 million fund to resolve a class action lawsuit alleging the sale of misleading U Coverage policies to motorists in New Mexico. According to NS3.AI, the settlement pertains to policies purchased between January 1, 2010, and December 31, 2021. Affected policyholders must submit their claims by July 2.

data.title: Ethereum and Chainlink Whales Accumulate Ahead of U.S. CPI Release
data.description: According to NS3.AI, recent data from Santiment reveals that Ethereum whale wallets have accumulated 500,000 ETH, valued at approximately $1.09 billion, just before the release of the U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI). In a similar move, Chainlink whale wallets have added 1.89 million LINK, worth about $16.93 million. Meanwhile, Nansen data indicates that Uniswap whale wallets have reduced their holdings by 2.48% over the past week, equivalent to around 90,000 UNI, as traders brace for potential volatility in response to the upcoming inflation report.

data.title: Binance to Adjust Tick Sizes for USDⓈ-M Perpetual Futures Contracts
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the exchange is set to adjust the tick size for several USDⓈ-M Perpetual Futures Contracts to enhance market liquidity and improve trading experiences. The adjustments will occur on specific dates, starting from April 13, 2026, at 06:30 (UTC). The trading pairs affected include BASEDUSDT, GTCUSDT, ENAUSDT, and ENAUSDC, with the tick size changing from 0.0001 to 0.00001. Further adjustments will follow on April 15 and April 16, 2026, impacting pairs such as GUAUSDT, SIRENUSDT, CHRUSDT, ALICEUSDT, DENTUSDT, OGNUSDT, and DYDXUSDT.

The adjustment will not affect the trading operations of USDⓈ-M Futures, except for specific contracts like CHRUSDT, ALICEUSDT, DENTUSDT, OGNUSDT, and DYDXUSDT. During the adjustment period, trading functions for these contracts will be temporarily suspended. For instance, the USDⓈ-M CHRUSDT contract will be suspended from April 16, 2026, 06:30 (UTC) to 06:31 (UTC), while the ALICEUSDT contract will be suspended from 06:45 (UTC) to 07:46 (UTC). Similar suspensions will occur for DENTUSDT, OGNUSDT, and DYDXUSDT contracts. During these times, users will be unable to place new orders or modify existing ones, and the contract status will change from "TRADING" to "PENDING_TRADING." API users can query the latest status and tick size changes using GET /fapi/v1/exchangeInfo.

Binance emphasizes that the tick size update will not affect existing orders, as orders placed before the update will still be matched with the original tick size. Users are advised to refer to the Trading Parameters for more details and adjust their trading strategies accordingly to avoid unnecessary impacts. This notice is released under Binance Exchange Rule 17, and users are encouraged to stay informed through the API Changelog for further updates.

data.title: BlackRock's Bitcoin Trust Sees Significant Inflows Amid Market Dynamics
data.description:
Investors directed $269.3 million into BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust on Thursday, marking its most successful day since early March, coinciding with the escalation of the US-Iran conflict. According to Cointelegraph, this influx helped reverse two days of net outflows among the 12 U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs, culminating in a net inflow of $358.1 million. Bitcoin ETF inflows serve as a measure of both retail and institutional interest in Bitcoin.

The Fidelity Wise Origin Bitcoin Fund (FBTC) followed with $53.3 million in inflows, while the newly launched Morgan Stanley Bitcoin Trust (MSBT) contributed $14.9 million on its second trading day, as reported by Farside Investors. Bitwise and ARK 21Shares' Bitcoin ETFs saw inflows of $11.7 million and $4.8 million, respectively, while Franklin Templeton and VanEck’s Bitcoin products recorded around $2 million. Since March 23, BlackRock’s IBIT has accumulated $1.5 billion in net inflows, despite a broader crypto market downturn that saw Bitcoin's price drop from a 2026 high of $97,000 to $72,100.

Robert Mitchnick, BlackRock’s digital assets head, remarked in March that IBIT investors tend to be "disproportionately long-term buy and hold" investors, even amid significant selling pressure in the Bitcoin market. Meanwhile, Amy Oldenburg, Morgan Stanley’s digital asset head, highlighted in a Bloomberg interview that MSBT was the bank’s most successful ETF launch to date. She noted, "This is just the first of a long roadmap of new products on the asset management side." Morgan Stanley has also filed to list a staked Ether (ETH) ETF and Solana (SOL) ETF.

With the recent inflows, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs are nearing a year-to-date net inflow. The Bitcoin ETFs concluded 2025 with $56.59 billion in net inflows and currently stand at $56.51 billion, just $80 million shy of returning to their initial inflow figures for the year.

data.title: Swiss Consumer Confidence Index Falls Sharply in March
data.description: Switzerland's consumer confidence index dropped significantly in March, reaching -42.9, according to Jin10. This figure is notably lower than the anticipated -32.4 and the previous value of -30.4. The decline suggests increasing pessimism among Swiss consumers regarding the economic outlook.

data.title: A newly created wallet deposited 5 million USDC into Hyperliquid to buy HYPE; 69,200 HYPE have already been purchased.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, whales continue to buy HYPE. A newly created wallet deposited 5 million USDC into Hyperliquid to buy HYPE, and has so far purchased 59,200 HYPE, worth $2.39 million.

data.title: Binance: The Alpha airdrop can be claimed at 17:00 today, with a minimum requirement of 240 Alpha Credits.
data.description: Binance will launch the Binance Alpha Airdrop today at 17:00 (UTC+8). This event uses the newly upgraded Binance Alpha Box model, with the airdrop pool containing tokens from multiple projects. Users with at least 240 Binance Alpha Points can claim a token reward on a first-come, first-served basis. Furthermore, this airdrop has three reward tiers: Common (60% of the reward pool), Rare (35%), and Super Rare (5%), each with a different Alpha Box value. Users will be automatically assigned to one of the tiers upon claiming. Rewards will be distributed proportionally based on the tier. If rewards are not fully distributed, the points threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes.
